channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Genel to Acquire Capricorn for $360 Million
item.description: The merger will see the British company, whose production comes from Iraq's Kurdistan region, expand into Egypt in the Sahara desert.

item.title: Oil price declines continue following indirect US-Iran talks in Qatar
item.description: Oil prices declined for a third consecutive day on 2 July, Thursday, as indirect negotiations between the US and Iran concluded in Doha, Qatar, without progress towards a lasting peace agreement.

item.title: DOF wins contract for TotalEnergies’ FSO Unity Replacement Project
item.description: DOF Group has been awarded a contract by TotalEnergies for subsea construction, mooring, and the replacement of a floating storage and offloading unit (FSO) as part of the FSO Unity Replacement Project.

item.title: Which Countries Produce and Consume the Most Natural Gas?
item.description: Rigzone examines the Energy Institute's latest statistical review of world energy report, which was released recently.

item.title: Petronas Makes New Discoveries offshore Suriname
item.description: Malaysia's national oil and gas company said it has now drilled 8 successful wells with combined recoverable resources of over 1 billion barrels of oil equivalent in the South American country.

item.title: Harbour Energy begins gas production at Dvalin North in Norwegian Sea
item.description: Harbour Energy has commenced gas production from the Dvalin North field in the Norwegian Sea, marking the start-up ahead of schedule and under budget.

item.title: Nigeria's NNPC Revenue Drops in May Despite Higher Oil Output
item.description: NNPC Limited booked lower revenues in May compared to April despite the higher crude oil and condensate production, the latest monthly figures by the Nigeria's state oil and gas firm showed. NNPC's revenues fell to…

item.title: Iran Struggles to Woo Oil Buyers before US Waiver Expires
item.description: A hoard of Iranian oil is building up at sea, as the Islamic Republic struggles to find buyers before the expiry of a 60-day window granted by Washington.

item.title: India Fast-Tracks State Company Stake Sales to Cover Oil Shock Costs
item.description: India is accelerating a plan to sell stakes in major state-held companies as it looks to raise billions of U.S. dollars to plug the gap left by the oil shock from the Strait of Hormuz closure. The Indian government has…

item.title: Japanese Banks Commit $496 Million to India's Power Grid Expansion
item.description: Japanese banks are set to provide financing of up to the equivalent of $496 million for a key grid transmission project in India under a new Asian framework on energy cooperation, Nikkei Asia reported on Thursday…

item.title: Saudi Arabia Rushes Crude Exports Through Reopened Strait of Hormuz
item.description: About 10 million barrels of Saudi crude have cleared the Strait of Hormuz in recent days, as supertankers continue to load oil from the Saudi port of Ras Tanura in the Persian Gulf and Saudi Arabia is ramping up oil…

item.title: AI Boom Drives Record Surge in Battery Demand
item.description: Battery demand has soared in recent months as developers of data centers and AI computing are increasingly relying on battery energy storage to manage power surges, executives at battery start-ups have told the…

item.title: Chinese Teapots Snap Up Middle East Crude As Prices Slide
item.description: Chinese private refiners are buying Middle Eastern crude on the spot market amid falling prices, Bloomberg reported today, citing two purchases of Saudi and Emirati crude. According to the report, one teapot refiner…

item.title: EU Bows to Tech Lobby, Drops Ten-Year Renewables Rule for Data Centers
item.description: The European Union has given in to pressure from Big Tech to water down its strict low-carbon energy requirements in order to pursue its AI agenda as the U.S. and China pull far ahead and the EU plays catchup. Citing a…

item.title: Iran Exports Crude at 20% Premium Even as Unsold Barrels Pile Up at Sea
item.description: Crude oil in floating storage off the Iranian coast is on the rise amid reluctance among buyers, Bloomberg reported today, citing data from Vortexa and its own calculations, suggesting some 58 million barrels are…

item.title: Saudis Make Rare Spot Oil Sales
item.description: Saudi Arabia is taking the unusual step of selling millions of barrels of oil on an ad-hoc basis to customers in Asia.

item.title: Crude Extends Losses on Glut Fears
item.description: Oil prices fell as recovering Strait of Hormuz traffic and rising global supplies reinforced expectations of an oversupplied market.

item.title: 5 Under-the-Radar Stocks Fueling the AI Revolution
item.description: Nvidia was the first poster child of the AI revolution. OpenAI turned ChatGPT into a global phenomenon. Google, Microsoft, and Anthropic have since dominated headlines with ever-more-powerful models. But throughout…

item.title: Trump Targets California Again In SpaceX Feud
item.description: For years, U.S. President Donald Trump has loomed large over California's energy and environmental sectors as he tries to promote fossil fuels and roll back the state’s climate initiatives, with varying degrees of…

item.title: Wall Street Is Pivoting From Solar To Solar Plus Storage
item.description: The global solar energy boom is showing no signs of slowing down, with falling costs and ambitious clean energy targets continuing to drive rapid adoption. Last year, solar capacity additions surged 11% Y/Y to a record…
